cards.back: A direct mapping is a cache mapping scheme where each memory address in the main memory is mapped directly to a specific cache location
An indirect mapping is a cache mapping scheme where each memory address is mapped to a set of cache locations, with a tag being used to determine which location is used
A set-associative mapping is a cache mapping scheme where each cache line is part of a set, and the cache controller uses a tag to determine which set to access
A cache line is a group of words in the cache memory, typically 64 bytes in size, that are stored together to improve performance
A cache tag is a unique identifier used to identify a cache line and determine which cache location to access for a given memory address
